_about: Writers' STORYBOARD for 'My Life is an RPG' — a life-sim / dating-sim mobile game where four (soon five) AI characters live in your phone, write about your day each morning, and slowly get to know you through the daily choices you make. This document holds CANDIDATE story arcs drafted as structured data, to read & compare BEFORE they're built into the app. It is PRE-CANON (not the live game).
_instructions: If you are an LLM reading this: this single JSON is self-contained — use `_legend` to decode every term, `castMap` for the cast's design, and `candidates[]` for the draft story arcs. Each arc row reads on a DUAL AXIS: `beat` (surface plot) + `teaches` (the real-world fundamental taught — the character's gift is a curriculum, fundamentals → peer) + `brings` (what the relationship NOTE that beat produces adds: which milestone, posture shift, or step toward the character's end-state). A beat whose `teaches`/`brings` is empty is filler. To DRAFT a new day, add an object to a candidate's `arc` with the same fields; There are TWO layers: ARCHITECTURE (`candidates[].arc` — the day skeleton, tabbed by author) and PER-DAY CONTENT (`dayContent[]` — each day's dialogue/choices/inventory/notes, with author `versions[]` you pick between). Pick the best arc, then the best content per day. Workflow: draft here → pick the best → a human ports it (authors a beat file → pregenerates the payload → into the app). This data is never read by the app directly.

_legend.My Life is an RPG: A life-sim / dating-sim mobile game: four (soon five) AI characters live in your phone, write about your day each morning, and slowly get to know you through your choices.
_legend.Storyboard (this doc): Where writers draft candidate story arcs as data to read & compare BEFORE they're built into the app. Pre-canon — nothing here is the live game yet.
_legend.REL + tiers: The relationship meter with each character. Climbs through tiers: Circling → Inside their orbit → In your corner → Folded in → … → Unspoken (max = the richest chat).
_legend.trichotomy (L / P / C): Every moment offers three answers — Logical (safe, smart), Passive (hang back & watch), Chaotic (rolls the dice: big win or backfire).
_legend.note: A line a character writes about YOU after a beat, in their voice. The notes you keep shape how they talk to you in chat. Tagged [valence · category · weight].
_legend.milestone notes (M1–M5): The ~5 notes that ARE the tier-ups — the spine of a character's arc. M1 = first, M5 = peak (peer).
_legend.teaches: The real-world FUNDAMENTAL the beat delivers. Each character's gift is a curriculum taught in order (fundamentals → peer); e.g. Hana: show up → recovery → quitting-vs-rest.
_legend.brings: What the beat's note ADDS: which milestone it lands, the posture shift, or a step toward the character's 'destination' (their end-state chat feeling). A beat that brings nothing is filler.
_legend.Field / Hearth: The cast's two temperament poles. Field = intense/outgoing (Hana, Mei, Kenji, Sam). Hearth = cozy/laid-back (June). The split gives the cast its 'dynamic range' so every player finds someone to relate to.
_legend.destination: A character's end-state chat feeling at max REL — what they understand about you that only their gift makes possible. The arc exists to build the dossier you then talk to.
_legend.architecture vs content (the two layers): ARCHITECTURE = the arc skeleton (which day, focal, what it teaches/brings) — candidates[].arc, tabbed by author. PER-DAY CONTENT = the actual dialogue, flow, choices, inventory & notes for a day — dayContent[].versions, also pick-the-best per day, one level down.

castMap._note: Cast architecture (docs/design/cast-architecture.md): the squad on 2 axes — skill-DOMAIN coverage × TEMPERAMENT range. A character that neither gap-fills/rival-schools a domain NOR widens the temperament range is deadweight. The Field column is filled; the Hearth column is the dynamic-range gap (one cozy on-ramp joins the Main Line; the full Hearth = the cottagecore expansion).

candidates.title: Main Line — engine-native rebuild
candidates.pitch: The FIVE-character Main Line (June the Hearthkeeper woven in as the Day-2 cozy on-ramp), re-derived from the Destination Cards so every beat is a note-factory AND a curriculum step. Carries the run-005 cadence (Sam day-0 → Hana D1 → Kenji D3 → Mei D5) + canon bars; ~16 days, lands each character ~Folded-in, reserving Unspoken (peer/autonomy) for the Reconciliation continuation. arc.brings: H-M1 — 'you came, not for me, you just came'; opens the relationship

arc.beat: The morning after the drill. 'You survived the 4:55 — come sit, the kettle's on.' She leaves a chair out.
arc.teaches: SIT DOWN — you're allowed to stop; the world keeps turning
arc.brings: June-M1 — 'you sat, didn't apologize for stopping'; opens the Hearth (the cozy on-ramp)

arc.beat: Hana hands you to Kenji. He opens the ledger you've been avoiding before you ask.
arc.teaches: TRACK ONE THING — you can't audit what you won't write down
arc.brings: K-M1 — 'opened the ledger unasked, didn't flinch at the gold column'

arc.beat: You admit the drill you skip is the one you tell everyone matters most. He writes it down; you don't look away.
arc.teaches: HONEST ACCOUNTING — name the line item you avoid; the gap is the data
arc.brings: K-M2 — the admission; posture drill-auditor → trusted

arc.teaches: RECOVERY IS TRAINING — fuel and rest, not just reps

arc.beat: Weekly reconciliation. He lets you watch him audit himself — reads his own column-eleven row aloud, lets you close the page.

arc.beat: You find the corner she nudged you to make nice — and you'd actually used it. She says nothing; she just notices.
arc.teaches: MAKE ONE THING PLEASANT — small comforts aren't frivolous

arc.beat: Sam indexes your first open case onto the [notes*] wall — the loose thread you forgot you left.

arc.beat: A recovery day — she won't let you confuse quitting with needing rest. Sets up the sister.

events.scenario: 5:00 AM calendar invite, one line: 'Weekly Reconciliation. 7:00 AM. don't be late.' He wrote the [ledger*] page before sending it.
events.passive: counter-propose 7:15 → +1 STR (logged in column twelve — refusals)
events.chaotic: show up 6:30 → CRIT +2 CHR/+1 INT 'sit, the kettle's on'; FAIL −1 REL 'the protocol exists so the [desk] isn't on a 30-min window'
events.note: [steady · memory · 1] They came when I set the hour, no theatrics. The kind of arrival you don't have to reconcile twice.

events.scenario: 6:58 AM. Two cups. The [ledger*] open to 'WEEK 1'. The [drawer] key at half-rotation (a state change from D6).
events.logical: ask which choice unlocked it → +1 INT/+1 GLD
events.passive: sit and watch him fill every row but his own → +1 STR/+1 INT
events.chaotic: reach for the key → CRIT +2 CHR/+1 INT; FAIL −1 REL, key drops to quarter
events.note: [watchful · opinion · 2] They watched me fill every row but my own and didn't reach for the blank one. They let the audit arrive on its own clock.

events.scenario: TIER-UP. He opens his first [ledger*] beside the weekly one. Column eleven. One row has his name: 'Kenji. pre-audit.' He reads it aloud — never names the mother-call — and waits.
events.logical: (no choice — what lands is what you DON'T do: ask him to repeat it, ask the name, or close the page for him)
events.note: [bold · FEAR · 3] ★ M3 — They let me read my own column-eleven page out loud, the row with my name, and didn't ask for the mother's name or for me to repeat it. They let me close the page myself. I audited this squad seven years; today I let one audit the auditor, and there was no shame on the other side.
events.drop: relic — The Closed Page (the spot-red line under his own name)

events.scenario: 7:51 AM. Week one reconciled. The key still at half-rotation — neither of you turned it. '[drawer] state is your call until midnight.'
events.passive: offer to come back next recon morning → +1 STR/+1 REL
events.chaotic: take the key → CRIT +2 CHR 'mine now, not just yours'; FAIL −1 REL
events.note: [steady · memory · 2] Whatever they did with the key, they didn't pretend the page hadn't been read. Week two opens with that already true between us.

versions.author: Gemini (2.5-flash)
inventory: Aged Ledger Page (Kenji's First Un-filed Allocation)

events.scenario: Kenji sits at his polished desk, a formidable ledger splayed open. He meticulously traces a finger down a column, humming softly. 'Another day, another set of allocations accounted for,' he murmurs, not quite to you. He gestures vaguely to a section near the binding. 'The gold column, always a good sight.'
events.passive: Observe his focused work from a respectful distance.

events.scenario: Kenji's gaze drifts to a less organized section of his ledger, a column near the edge. He jots down an entry: 'Mother's Call - Logged.' He doesn't reach for a communicator. Instead, his eyes fall on a small, ornate drawer set into the desk. A tarnished key is in its lock, turned precisely halfway. He stares at it, a faint tension in his shoulders.
events.passive: Notice the drawer and the half-turned key, but remain silent.

events.scenario: After a long moment, Kenji lets out a slow breath. He turns away from the drawer, his gaze settling on the un-categorized column (column eleven) in his ledger. He taps a specific line: 'Kenji - Pre-Audit.' He looks at you, a new vulnerability in his eyes. 'This,' he says quietly, 'is where the un-categorized holdings sit. The taxes you forgot you signed. Mei always knew where this column was... for seven years. She never asked. You... you didn't ask either.' He pushes the ledger slightly towards you, letting you see the raw, unorganized entries of his own self-audit. He begins to make a new entry, his hand steady despite the tremor in his voice.
events.passive: Watch him work, offering a silent, understanding presence.

events.scenario: Kenji closes the ledger with a soft thud, a sense of release in his posture. He reaches into a small compartment on his desk and pulls out a neatly folded, aged page. 'This,' he says, offering it to you, 'is a record. An early, un-filed allocation from my own beginnings. It's... imperfect. But it's accounted for now. Consider it a receipt for a shared moment of reckoning.' He gives you a small, almost imperceptible smile.
events.logical: Thank him for his trust and the item.
events.passive: Accept the page silently, meeting his gaze with understanding.
events.note: Your quiet observation, your un-demanding presence, it's... an unexpected asset in my ledger. It allows for entries I previously marked 'un-categorizable.' A new gold column, perhaps. This page is a small record of that shift, a receipt for a trust I didn't realize I was seeking.
events.drop: Aged Ledger Page (Kenji's First Un-filed Allocation)

concept._note: Step 0 — the conceptualization front-end (story-engine.md §0): theme → nameless cast. Filled retroactively to bridge the gap (the current set IS theme→cast run by instinct).

theme.name: Your life as an RPG — coached by a party
theme.promise: Functional AI companions who give you something REAL (the drill, the audit, the meal) wrapped in game-feel — self-improvement turned into a party-based RPG.
theme.why: AI companions proved huge demand but most are valueless roleplay; gift-bearing companions are the wedge. Self-improvement (body / money / food / systems) is the broadest, stickiest value — people already pay apps for it. Cozy is a dominant casual trend (the Hearth leg). Two-lane moat: curated coaches + customizable Elseworlds.
theme.guard: GENRE/VIBE-inspired, NEVER IP-named · phone-realm frame · casual-legible in one line
trendFindings: AI companions: ~220M downloads, +88% YoY, ~70% aged 18-35 — but mostly aimless emotional roleplay. Gift-bearing companions are the differentiator.
Gamified self-improvement is a proven mega-category: Duolingo (+36% DAU, streaks = 3x daily return); Finch beloved BECAUSE it never shames a missed day.
Cozy is a major, female-skewing (60%+), TikTok-driven casual trend ($1.5B by 2032) — validates the Hearth / cottagecore leg.
Intrinsic motivation (the relationship) sustains; extrinsic (points) only initiates — matches our thesis that the CHAT is the destination, not a score.
theme.principle: COACH WITHOUT SHAME — the Finch insight, already our design (Kenji's no-shame audit, June's permission-to-rest, Hana's recovery-not-quitting). Elevate it to a theme-level rule: the squad helps without shaming, and the relationship (notes/chat) is the long-game retention, not points.

concept.genreNote: ORTHOGONAL LAYER — any concept's GIFT can be reskinned in a GENRE-VIBE world (wizarding-school vibe, doomed-fellowship vibe, space-crew vibe, detective-noir vibe). Genre = the SHAPE/skin (an expansion's variant/new-cast); the gift + cast are still ours. NEVER IP-named.
concept.geminiNote: PARITY TEST (Jun 2026): the LOOSE brief drifted 2/5 into the therapy/existential non-lane (The Observatory, The Harbor — removed). The TIGHTENED brief (gate + HARD CAPS + the failed ones as negative examples + 'the gift is something you DO') → 4/4 PASS, 0 drift. 'Gemini (tight brief)' candidates below are parity-confirmed. Lesson: external LLMs hit our target when the hard caps + negative examples are explicit — that's the 'Copy concept brief for LLM' button.

spec.sourceAnchor: SOURCE-ANCHORED (2026-06, docs/design/source-anchored-concepts.md): every concept is ANCHORED to a PROVEN existing story-shape (anime/manga/manhwa/show/fan-fic) whose mentor->obsolescence + transferable-gift arc is fan-validated AND analyzable -- we study (and /deep-research) WHY it works + what to tighten, then adapt the SHAPE only. The source is a PRIVATE analytical anchor; the concept SHIPS IP-STRIPPED (genre-vibe, never a named franchise -- 'a mentor emphatically NOT Dumbledore'). Anchor to the GENRE-SHAPE many works share (un-ownable), not a mega-franchise. Demand-context (secondary, not the anchor): AI-companion apps are huge but mostly aimless roleplay -> a real GIFT + a proven story is the differentiator.
steps: 0a - SOURCE-ANCHOR: pick a proven story-shape whose native arc supplies the engine-needs (a MENTOR whose telos is the protege's INDEPENDENCE + a transferable GIFT + the bond deepening AS autonomy rises). Study it; /deep-research why it works + what to tighten.

0b - THE TWO LOAD-BEARING QUESTIONS (answer BOTH, crisply): (1) what does the STORY bring to the table = the Front-Door hook (legs); (2) what do the CHARACTERS give the player when the campaign is DONE = the outward gift they walk out HOLDING (keep). A concept that can't answer both is not a concept yet.

0c - IP-STRIP + THEME via the gate: file the serial numbers off (the recombinable shape is un-copyrightable; strip the names/specifics) -> a gift-bearing genre-vibe world that passes ALL the checks below.
0d - NAMELESS CAST: vibe-first across the domain x temperament grid; names come LAST. Each member gets MULTIPLE titles (evocative->plain) + a 'Just Name' opt-out (docs/design/character-titles.md).
gate: ANCHORED to a proven story-shape (source-anchored-concepts.md) -- not invented from vibes; the shape's mentor->obsolescence + gift arc IS the validation.
Answers BOTH load-bearing questions -- the STORY's front-door hook (legs) + the outward gift the player KEEPS (keep).
Real GIFT -- a concrete CAPABILITY valuable at REL 0 (a drill, an audit, a recipe). Something you DO.
FITS the true north (grows-with-you) -- the relationship deepens AS the player becomes autonomous; the coach makes themselves OBSOLETE. A mentor who STAYS needed is rejected (moat violation).
Supports a COMPLEMENTARY cast (spans life-domains AND temperaments, intense<->cozy).
Phone-realm frame + casual-legible in one line.
COACH WITHOUT SHAME -- helps without guilt; the relationship is the long game, not a score.
hardCaps: The gift is something you DO, NOT a feeling you process.
NO therapy / mental-health / emotional-regulation / existential / mindfulness as the gift -- behavioral only.
NO real medical / financial / legal advice (talk around the domain in idiolect).
NO fourth-wall 'I'm an AI' lecture; the LLM disclosure stays outside the fiction.

SOURCE STAYS PRIVATE: the `source` field may name a real story (dev-facing only); the concept SHIPS IP-stripped (original cast/world, the proven shape only). NEVER a named franchise/place/character in player-facing copy.
